rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
Preserve AADL scope rules: component ports and data types must be valid in the target scope.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers and types present in the input AADL model.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or properties not declared in the model.
Ensure AGREE expressions align with AADL data types and port directions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports in valid scopes.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax notes for component type vs implementation annexes
AADL scope and owner rules for AGREE contracts
Defensive rules for Boolean data range comparisons
Defensive rules for AADL property name usage in AGREE
Defensive rules for event port numeric encoding
